`dim1` / `dim2` rotation).  Non-diagonal positions are zero.

members.name: i0
members.summary: Modified Bessel function of the first kind, order 0: $I_0(x)$.
members.extended:
Composite implementation via the Abramowitz & Stegun polynomial
approximation (Table 9.8.1):

* `|x| <= 3.75` — polynomial series in $(x / 3.75)^2$.
* `|x| >  3.75` — polynomial series in $3.75 / |x|$, multiplied
  by $e^{|x|} / \sqrt{|x|}$ to recover the exponential growth.

Differentiable through both branches (engine primitives only).

parameters.name: x
parameters.description: Real input.

returns.description: $I_0(x)$ element-wise; always positive.

members.name: frexp
members.summary:
Decompose `input` into mantissa `m` and exponent `e` such that
`input = m * 2**e` with `|m|` in `[0.5, 1)` (or `m == 0`).
members.extended:
Returns a `(mantissa, exponent)` tuple.  Exponent dtype is int32.
Non-finite inputs propagate: NaN → (NaN, 0); ±inf → (±inf, 0); 0 → (0, 0).

members.name: gcd
members.summary: Element-wise greatest common divisor for integer tensors.
members.extended:
Falls through to Python's `math.gcd` per element — the engine
has no native GCD kernel and this path is for low-rate utility use
(e.g. shape arithmetic), not hot-loop computation.  Non-differentiable.

parameters.name: x
parameters.description: Same-shape integer tensors.

parameters.name: y
parameters.description: Same-shape integer tensors.

returns.description:
Same shape and dtype as `x`.  Each element is
`math.gcd(x_i, y_i)`.

members.name: lgamma
members.summary: Natural log of the gamma function $\ln \Gamma(x)$.
members.extended:
Implemented as a pure composite over engine primitives — uses the
Lanczos approximation (Numerical Recipes, $g = 7$, 9 series
coefficients).  Relative error stays below `1.5e-15` for real
`x > 0` in float64.  Autograd flows through automatically because
every step is an engine op.

parameters.name: x
parameters.description:
Real input.  Defined for `x > 0`; behaviour on non-positive
reals is implementation-defined (the series diverges).

returns.description: Same shape as `x`.

members.name: erfc
members.summary: Complementary error function $\text{erfc}(x) = 1 - \text{erf}(x)$.
members.extended:
Preserves more precision than `1 - erf(x)` would for very large
positive `x` only if the engine offers a native `erfc` kernel;
in Lucid this is a composite that *does* subtract `erf(x)` from
one, so accuracy is bounded by `erf` near the tails.

parameters.name: x
parameters.description: Real-valued input.

returns.description: Same shape as `x`, values in $[0, 2]$.

members.name: digamma
members.summary: Digamma function $\psi(x) = \frac{d}{dx} \ln \Gamma(x)$.
members.extended:
Implemented as a composite of engine primitives so autograd flows
through naturally.  Uses the standard recurrence-plus-asymptotic
strategy: shift the argument by 8 via
$\psi(x) = \psi(x + 8) - \sum_{k=0}^{7} 1/(x + k)$, then
evaluate the asymptotic Bernoulli series on the shifted argument
where the series converges quickly.

parameters.name: x
parameters.description: Real input.  Defined for `x > 0`.

returns.description: Same shape as `x`.

members.name: copysign
members.summary: Element-wise sign transplant: magnitudes from `x`, signs from `y`.
members.extended:
Useful for sign-preserving operations that need to preserve a sign
bit independent of an arbitrary magnitude computation.

parameters.name: x
parameters.description: Magnitude source; result has `|x|` element-wise.

parameters.name: y
parameters.description:
Sign source; result is positive where `y >= 0`, negative
where `y < 0` (broadcastable to the shape of `x`).

returns.description: Broadcast shape of `x` and `y`.

members.name: ldexp
members.summary: Element-wise $\text{input} \cdot 2^{\text{exponent}}$.
members.extended:
Differentiable through both arguments (uses `exp * log(2)`
internally rather than integer bit twiddling), so safe to chain
inside autograd graphs.

parameters.name: input
parameters.description: Mantissa.

parameters.name: exponent
parameters.description: Power-of-two exponent; broadcastable to the shape of `input`.

returns.description: Broadcast shape of `input` and `exponent`.

members.name: logdet
members.summary: Log-determinant of a square matrix (or batch).
members.extended:
Returns `log(det(A))`.  Defined only for matrices with `det > 0`;
returns NaN otherwise.  Uses `linalg.slogdet` internally.

members.name: tril_indices
members.signature: tril_indices(row: int, col: int | None = None, offset: int = 0, dtype: DTypeLike = None, device: DeviceLike = None)
members.summary: Indices of the lower-triangular part of an `(row, col)` matrix.

parameters.name: row
parameters.description: Number of rows in the matrix being indexed.

parameters.name: col
parameters.description:
Number of columns.  `None` (default) → `col = row`
(square matrix).

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description:
Diagonal offset.  `0` (default) = main diagonal,
positive = include diagonals above it, negative = exclude
diagonals below it.  Indexing keeps positions where
`j - i <= offset`.

parameters.name: dtype
parameters.description: Output dtype (default: int64).

parameters.name: device
parameters.description: Placement of the result.

returns.description:
Shape `(2, N)`.  Row `0` holds row indices, row `1` holds
column indices, in row-major order.

members.name: triu_indices
members.signature: triu_indices(row: int, col: int | None = None, offset: int = 0, dtype: DTypeLike = None, device: DeviceLike = None)
members.summary: Indices of the upper-triangular part of an `(row, col)` matrix.
members.extended:
Mirrors `tril_indices` with the inequality flipped — keeps
entries where `j - i >= offset`.

parameters.name: row
parameters.description: Number of rows in the matrix being indexed.

parameters.name: col
parameters.description: Number of columns.  `None` (default) → `col = row`.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description:
Diagonal offset.  `0` (default) = main diagonal,
positive = exclude diagonals close to the main one,
negative = include diagonals below it.

parameters.name: dtype
parameters.description: Output dtype (default: int64).

parameters.name: device
parameters.description: Placement of the result.

returns.description:
Shape `(2, N)`.  Row `0` holds row indices, row `1` holds
column indices, in row-major order.

members.name: combinations
members.signature: combinations(input: Tensor, r: int = 2, with_replacement: bool = False)
members.summary: All `r`-length combinations of the elements of a 1-D `input`.

parameters.name: input
parameters.description: 1-D source tensor of length $n$.  Raises on higher rank.

parameters.name: r
parameters.description: Combination length.  Default `2`.

parameters.name: with_replacement
parameters.description:
When `True` the output enumerates multisets (an element can
appear multiple times in a single row), giving
$\binom{n + r - 1}{r}$ rows.  Default `False` (strict
combinations, $\binom{n}{r}$ rows).

returns.description:
Shape `(C, r)` where $C$ is the combination count.
Dtype and device follow `input`.

members.name: var_mean
members.signature: var_mean(x: Tensor, dim: int | Sequence[int] | None = None, correction: int = 1, keepdim: bool = False)
members.summary: Return `(var, mean)` along `dim` in a single call.
members.extended:
Variance counterpart to `std_mean`.  Useful inside
normalisation layers where the squared deviation is the quantity
actually wanted (e.g. BatchNorm / LayerNorm forward computes
`(x - mean) / sqrt(var + eps)`).

parameters.name: x
parameters.description: Input.

parameters.name: dim
parameters.annotation: int, sequence of int, or None
parameters.description:
Axis or axes to reduce.  `None` (default) → full-tensor
reduction.

parameters.name: correction
parameters.description:
Bessel correction passed to `lucid.var`.  Default `1`
(unbiased sample variance).

parameters.name: keepdim
parameters.description:
Retain the reduced axes as size-1 dimensions.  Default
`False`.

returns.description: `(var, mean)`.

members.name: histogram
members.signature: histogram(input: Tensor, bins: int | Sequence[float] = 10, range: tuple[float, float] | None = None, density: bool = False, weight: Tensor | None = None)
members.summary: Compute a 1-D histogram of `input` values.

parameters.name: input
parameters.description: Values to bin.  Flattened internally — shape doesn't matter.

parameters.name: bins
parameters.description:
When an int, the number of equal-width bins (default `10`).
When a sequence, the explicit bin edges (length `bins_n + 1`).

parameters.name: range
parameters.description:
`(lo, hi)`.  Values outside the range are dropped.  Ignored
when `bins` is a sequence of explicit edges.  `None`
(default) → `(input.min(), input.max())`.

parameters.name: density
parameters.description:
When `True` divide by total count × bin width to yield a
probability density that integrates to 1.  Default `False`.

parameters.name: weight
parameters.description:
Same length as `input`.  When supplied, sums weights per bin
rather than counting.  Default `None`.

returns.description:
`(hist, bin_edges)`.  `hist` is a 1-D count/density tensor;
`bin_edges` is 1-D of length `bins + 1`.

members.name: histogramdd
members.signature: histogramdd(input: Tensor, bins: int | Sequence[int] = 10, range: Sequence[tuple[float, float]] | None = None, density: bool = False)
members.summary: N-dimensional histogram for arbitrary sample dimension.

parameters.name: input
parameters.description:
Shape `(N, D)` — $N$ samples in $D$
dimensions.  Other ranks raise.

parameters.name: bins
parameters.description:
Bin count per axis.  A single int applies to every axis; a
sequence of length $D$ sets each axis independently.
Default `10`.

parameters.name: range
parameters.description:
Per-axis `(lo, hi)` ranges.  When `None` (default) each
axis uses its own `(min, max)` of the samples.

parameters.name: density
parameters.description:
When `True` divide by total count × bin volume to yield a
density that integrates to 1.  Default `False`.

returns.description:
`(counts, edges)`.  `counts` shape
`(bins_0, …, bins_{D-1})`; `edges` is a length-$D$
list of 1-D tensors of size `bins_i + 1`.

members.name: bincount
members.signature: bincount(input: Tensor, weights: Tensor | None = None, minlength: int = 0)
members.summary: Count occurrences of each integer value in `input`.

parameters.name: input
parameters.description: 1-D non-negative integer tensor.  Negative values raise.

parameters.name: weights
parameters.description:
Same length as `input`.  When supplied, `output[i]` is the
*sum of weights* for entries with value `i` (rather than the
plain count).  Default `None`.

parameters.name: minlength
parameters.description:
Floor for the output length.  The output has length
`max(input.max() + 1, minlength)`.  Default `0`.

returns.description:
1-D tensor; dtype is float when `weights` is given, otherwise
int64.

members.name: poisson
members.summary: Sample element-wise from $\mathrm{Poisson}(\text{rate} = \text{input}[i])$.

parameters.name: input
parameters.description:
Non-negative rates $\lambda_i$ per output position.  Any
shape.

parameters.name: generator
parameters.description:
Override Lucid's default Philox generator with an isolated
stream.  When `None` (default), draws are controlled by
`manual_seed`.

returns.description:
`int64` tensor with the same shape as `input`.  Zero rates
always return zero.

raises.description: If any element of `input` is negative.

notes:
Two-branch implementation:

* **Knuth's multiplication method** for `rate < 30` — exact but
  runtime grows with the rate.
* **Normal approximation** $\mathrm{Pois}(\lambda) \approx \lfloor \mathcal{N}(\lambda, \sqrt{\lambda}) + 0.5 \rfloor$
  for `rate >= 30` — bias < 0.05 standard deviations, orders of
  magnitude faster than Knuth in the tail.

members.name: corrcoef
members.summary: Pearson correlation-coefficient matrix.
members.extended:
Composite of `cov` divided by the outer product of the
per-variable standard deviations:

$$
R_{ij} = \frac{C_{ij}}{\sqrt{C_{ii}} \sqrt{C_{jj}}}.
$$

Diagonal entries are `1.0` (up to floating-point); off-diagonal
entries fall in $[-1, 1]$.

parameters.name: input
parameters.description:
Shape `(N,)` for a single-variable degenerate case, or
`(N, M)` for `N` variables observed at `M` time points.

returns.description: Shape `(N, N)` Pearson correlation matrix.

see_also.name: cov
see_also.description: raw covariance matrix without the std-normalisation.

members.name: cdist
members.signature: cdist(x1: Tensor, x2: Tensor, p: float = 2.0)
members.summary: Pairwise $L_p$ distance matrix between rows of `x1` and `x2`.
members.extended:
Specialised paths exist for `p` in $\{1, 2, \infty, 0\}$;
other `p` values fall back to the general
$(\sum_k |a_k - b_k|^p)^{1/p}$ formula.  For `p=2` the
numerically stable expansion
$\|a-b\|^2 = \|a\|^2 + \|b\|^2 - 2 a \cdot b^\top$ is used
so large coordinates don't lose precision.

parameters.name: x1
parameters.description: Shape `(..., P, M)`.

parameters.name: x2
parameters.description:
Shape `(..., R, M)`.  Leading batch dimensions are
broadcast-compatible with `x1`.

parameters.name: p
parameters.description:
$L_p$ exponent.  Default `2.0` (Euclidean).  Use
`float("inf")` for Chebyshev, `0.0` for the Hamming-style
non-zero count.

returns.description:
Shape `(..., P, R)`.  Entry $(\ldots, i, j)$ =
$\|x_1[\ldots, i, :] - x_2[\ldots, j, :]\|_p$.

members.name: histogram2d
members.signature: histogram2d(x: Tensor, y: Tensor, bins: int | tuple[int, int] = 10, range: tuple[tuple[float, float], tuple[float, float]] | None = None, density: bool = False)
members.summary: Joint 2-D histogram of paired observations `(x[i], y[i])`.

parameters.name: x
parameters.description: 1-D x-coordinates.

parameters.name: y
parameters.description: 1-D y-coordinates; must have the same length as `x`.

parameters.name: bins
parameters.description:
Bin count.  A single int applies to both axes; a 2-tuple sets
each axis independently.  Default `10` (i.e. 10×10).

parameters.name: range
parameters.description:
`((x_lo, x_hi), (y_lo, y_hi))`.  When `None` (default)
each axis uses its own `(min, max)`.

parameters.name: density
parameters.description:
When `True` divide by total count × bin area to yield a
probability density (sums × bin-area = 1).  Default `False`.

returns.description:
`(counts, x_edges, y_edges)`.  `counts` shape
`(bins_x, bins_y)` int64 (or float64 when `density`).
`x_edges` length `bins_x + 1`, `y_edges` length
`bins_y + 1`.

members.name: multinomial
members.signature: multinomial(input: Tensor, num_samples: int, replacement: bool = False, generator: _C_engine.Generator | None = None)
members.summary: Draw `num_samples` indices from the categorical distribution defined by `input`.
members.extended:
Non-differentiable — gradients of `input` are not propagated
through the sampling step.  Pass an explicit `generator` for an
isolated PRNG stream; without one, draws come from Lucid's
`manual_seed`-controlled default Philox generator.

parameters.name: input
parameters.description:
Probability weights.  1-D `(K,)` for a single categorical
distribution, or 2-D `(B, K)` for a batch of `B`
distributions.  Weights are renormalised internally; they need
not sum to 1.

parameters.name: num_samples
parameters.description: Number of draws per distribution.

parameters.name: replacement
parameters.description:
When `True` each draw is independent.  When `False`
(default) each chosen index is removed before the next draw
within the same row.

parameters.name: generator
parameters.description:
Override Lucid's default Philox generator with an isolated
stream.

returns.description:
Integer tensor.  Shape `(num_samples,)` for 1-D input,
`(B, num_samples)` for 2-D input.

members.name: std_mean
members.signature: std_mean(x: Tensor, dim: int | Sequence[int] | None = None, correction: int = 1, keepdim: bool = False)
members.summary: Return `(std, mean)` along `dim` in a single call.
members.extended:
Fused convenience over `lucid.std` + `lucid.mean` — the
engine reduction still scans `x` twice, but the call site is
shorter and the two outputs share an explicit `correction` /
`keepdim` contract.

parameters.name: x
parameters.description: Input.

parameters.name: dim
parameters.annotation: int, sequence of int, or None
parameters.description:
Axis or axes to reduce.  `None` (default) reduces over every
axis to a scalar pair.

parameters.name: correction
parameters.description:
Bessel correction passed to `lucid.std`.  `1` is the
unbiased sample std (default); `0` gives the biased
population std.

parameters.name: keepdim
parameters.description:
Retain the reduced axes as size-1 dimensions.  Default
`False`.

returns.description:
`(std, mean)` — same shape relationship as
`lucid.std` / `lucid.mean` would produce
individually.

members.name: quantile
members.signature: quantile(input: Tensor, q: float | Sequence[float] | Tensor, dim: int | None = None, keepdim: bool = False, interpolation: str = 'linear')
members.summary: Compute the *q*-th quantile of `input`.

parameters.name: input
parameters.description: Real-valued input.

parameters.name: q
parameters.annotation: float, sequence of float, or Tensor
parameters.description:
Quantile(s) in $[0, 1]$.  A single `q` returns a tensor
without the leading quantile axis; a sequence/tensor adds a
leading axis of length `len(q)`.

parameters.name: dim
parameters.description:
Axis along which to reduce.  `None` (default) reduces over
the flattened input.

parameters.name: keepdim
parameters.description:
Retain the reduced axis as a size-1 dimension.  Default
`False`.

parameters.name: interpolation
parameters.description:
Rule for interpolating between the two samples bracketing the
quantile.  One of `'linear'` (default), `'lower'`,
`'higher'`, `'midpoint'`, `'nearest'`.

returns.description:
Same dtype as `input`.  Shape depends on `q` / `dim` /
`keepdim`.

members.name: nanquantile
members.signature: nanquantile(input: Tensor, q: float | Sequence[float] | Tensor, dim: int | None = None, keepdim: bool = False, interpolation: str = 'linear')
members.summary:
NaN-aware quantile — same interface as `quantile`, but
every `NaN` is excluded from the sort and the per-axis quantile
is computed over the remaining values.

parameters.name: input
parameters.description:
Real-valued input.  `NaN` entries are masked out before
sorting.

parameters.name: q
parameters.annotation: float, sequence of float, or Tensor
parameters.description:
Quantile(s) in $[0, 1]$.  A single `q` returns a tensor
without the leading quantile axis; a sequence/tensor adds a
leading axis of length `len(q)`.

parameters.name: dim
parameters.description:
Axis along which to reduce.  `None` (default) reduces over
the flattened input.

parameters.name: keepdim
parameters.description:
Retain the reduced axis as a size-1 dimension.  Default
`False`.

parameters.name: interpolation
parameters.description:
Interpolation rule between two surrounding samples.  Currently
`"linear"` is supported (default).

returns.description:
Same dtype as `input`; shape depends on `q` / `dim` /
`keepdim`.  Returns `NaN` for slices that contain only
`NaN` values.

see_also.name: quantile
see_also.description: NaN-unaware version (a single `NaN` poisons the slice).

members.name: cov
members.summary: Covariance matrix of `input`.

parameters.name: input
parameters.description:
Shape `(N,)` (single-variable degenerate case) or `(N, M)`
— *N* variables observed at *M* time points.

parameters.name: correction
parameters.description:
Bessel correction; the divisor is `max(1, M - correction)`.
Default `1` (unbiased sample covariance); pass `0` for the
biased population covariance.

returns.description:
Shape `(N, N)`.  Entry $(i, j)$ =
$\frac{1}{M - \text{correction}}\sum_k (x_{i,k} - \bar x_i)(x_{j,k} - \bar x_j)$.

members.name: amin
members.signature: amin(x: Tensor, dim: int | Sequence[int] | None = None, keepdim: bool = False)
members.summary: Reduce `x` to per-slice minima along `dim` — values only.
members.extended:
Mirror image of `amax`.  Accepts a tuple of axes for a fused
multi-axis reduction; returns just the minimum values (never the
indices).

parameters.name: x
parameters.description: Input tensor.

parameters.name: dim
parameters.annotation: int, sequence of int, or None
parameters.description:
Axis or axes to reduce.  `None` (default) reduces over every
axis to a 0-D scalar.

parameters.name: keepdim
parameters.description:
Retain the reduced axes as size-1 dimensions.  Default
`False`.

returns.description: Minima along `dim`.

see_also.name: lucid.min
see_also.description: returns `(values, indices)` for a single axis.

members.name: count_nonzero
members.signature: count_nonzero(x: Tensor, dim: int | Sequence[int] | None = None)
members.summary: Count non-zero elements along `dim` (or whole tensor when `None`).

parameters.name: x
parameters.description:
Input.  Non-zero is determined by element-wise comparison to
zero (NaN counts as non-zero).

parameters.name: dim
parameters.annotation: int, sequence of int, or None
parameters.description:
Axis or axes to reduce.  `None` (default) reduces the entire
tensor to a 0-D scalar.

returns.description:
`int64` count(s).  Shape mirrors `lucid.sum` over the
same `dim` argument.

notes:
Composite implementation: the CPU backend lacks
`where(bool, i64, i64)` and `astype(bool → i64)`, so the op
builds a float32 `1.0/0.0` mask, reduces, and `astype`-casts
the float scalar back to int64 (a path the backend does support).
Slightly more allocations than a native int reduction but
semantically identical.

members.name: amax
members.signature: amax(x: Tensor, dim: int | Sequence[int] | None = None, keepdim: bool = False)
members.summary: Reduce `x` to per-slice maxima along `dim` — values only.
members.extended:
Multi-axis sibling of `lucid.max` that returns *only* the
reduced values (never the indices) and accepts a tuple of axes
for a single fused reduction.  Use when you don't need the
`(values, indices)` tuple `lucid.max` returns.

parameters.name: x
parameters.description: Input tensor.

parameters.name: dim
parameters.annotation: int, sequence of int, or None
parameters.description:
Axis or axes to reduce.  `None` (default) reduces over every
axis and returns a 0-D scalar tensor.

parameters.name: keepdim
parameters.description:
Retain the reduced axes as size-1 dimensions.  Default
`False`.

returns.description: Maxima along `dim`; shape depends on `dim` and `keepdim`.

see_also.name: lucid.max
see_also.description: returns `(values, indices)` for a single axis.

members.name: index_copy
members.signature: index_copy(input: Tensor, dim: int, index: Tensor, source: Tensor)
members.summary: Return a copy of `input` with slices at `index` replaced by `source`.
members.extended:
Composite implementation: first zeroes the indexed slices via
`index_fill`, then adds `source` via `index_add`.
Differentiable through both `input` and `source`.

parameters.name: input
parameters.description: Destination tensor; not mutated (a fresh copy is returned).

parameters.name: dim
parameters.description: Axis along which slices are addressed.

parameters.name: index
parameters.description:
1-D `int32` / `int64` tensor of positions along `dim`.
Length must equal `source.shape[dim]`.

parameters.name: source
parameters.description:
Replacement slices.  All non-`dim` dimensions must match
`input`; `source.shape[dim]` must equal `index.shape[0]`.

returns.description:
Same shape and dtype as `input`; values at the indexed
positions are taken from `source`, others from `input`.

members.name: index_fill
members.signature: index_fill(input: Tensor, dim: int, index: Tensor, value: float)
members.summary: Return a copy of `input` with positions `index` along `dim` set to `value`.
members.extended:
Autograd flows through the *unmasked* positions; filled positions
receive zero gradient (they're overwritten by a constant).

parameters.name: input
parameters.description: Source tensor; not mutated.

parameters.name: dim
parameters.description: Axis along which `index` addresses slices.

parameters.name: index
parameters.description: 1-D integer tensor of positions along `dim`.

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: Scalar to write into every indexed position.

returns.description:
Same shape and dtype as `input`; indexed slices replaced by
`value`, others unchanged.

members.name: masked_scatter
members.signature: masked_scatter(input: Tensor, mask: Tensor, source: Tensor)
members.summary: Copy elements from `source` into `input` at positions where `mask` is True.

members.name: index_add
members.signature: index_add(input: Tensor, dim: int, index: Tensor, source: Tensor, alpha: float = 1.0)
members.summary: Return `input` with `alpha * source` accumulated at `index` positions along `dim`.
members.extended:
Differentiable through both `input` and `source`.  `alpha` is
accumulated as a Python constant — gradients pass through cleanly
as if the multiplication were inlined.

parameters.name: input
parameters.description: Source tensor; not mutated.

parameters.name: dim
parameters.description: Axis along which `index` addresses slices.

parameters.name: index
parameters.description:
1-D integer tensor of length $m$ listing positions along
`dim` to accumulate into.

parameters.name: source
parameters.description:
Per-slice update tensor; same shape as `input` except
`source.shape[dim] == m` (matching `index` length).

parameters.name: alpha
parameters.description:
Scalar multiplier applied to `source` before accumulation.
Default `1.0`.

returns.description:
Same shape and dtype as `input`; positions listed in
`index` carry `input[..., index[i], ...] + alpha * source[..., i, ...]`.

members.name: index_put_
members.signature: index_put_(input: Tensor, indices: list[Tensor] | tuple[Tensor, ...], values: Tensor, accumulate: bool = False)
members.summary:
In-place variant of `index_put` — mutates `input` so the
write is visible through the same Tensor reference.
members.extended:
Internally this rebinds `input._impl` to the freshly-built result,
matching the convention Lucid already uses for engine-level in-place
ops (`add_`, `mul_`).  Storage-level mutation is not currently
available for composite indexing — autograd consumers should treat
the returned tensor as a new node.

parameters.name: input
parameters.description: Destination; mutated in place via `_impl` rebind.

parameters.name: indices
parameters.annotation: list of Tensor or tuple of Tensor
parameters.description:
Per-axis index tensors (one per dimension of `input`); same
contract as `index_put`.

parameters.name: values
parameters.description: Values to write at the addressed positions.

parameters.name: accumulate

returns.description: The same `input` tensor, now holding the updated values.

members.name: put
members.signature: put(input: Tensor, index: Tensor, source: Tensor, accumulate: bool = False)
members.summary: Write `source` into `input` at the *flat* positions in `index`.
members.extended:
Mirrors the reference framework's `Tensor.put` semantics: indices
refer to the row-major linearisation of `input`, regardless of its
shape.  `accumulate=True` performs additive scatter (duplicates
add), otherwise duplicates resolve to the last write (`scatter`
semantics).

parameters.name: input
parameters.description: Destination — its shape is preserved in the output.

parameters.name: index
parameters.description:
1-D (or flattenable) integer tensor of flat positions in
`[0, input.numel())`.

parameters.name: source
parameters.description:
Values to scatter; must be flattenable to the same length as
`index`.

parameters.name: accumulate
parameters.description:
If True, add to the existing value at each position; otherwise
overwrite.

members.name: index_put
members.signature: index_put(input: Tensor, indices: list[Tensor] | tuple[Tensor, ...], values: Tensor, accumulate: bool = False)
members.summary: Out-of-place advanced-indexing write.
members.extended:
Equivalent to `out = input.clone(); out[indices] = values` (or
`out[indices] += values` when `accumulate=True`) under reference
framework semantics.  `indices` is a sequence of integer tensors,
one per leading dimension; broadcasting between them follows the
standard rules.

Currently restricted to the case where `len(indices) ==
input.ndim` and every index tensor broadcasts to a common shape —
partial advanced indexing (where trailing dims are implicitly
sliced) is filed as a follow-up.

parameters.name: input
parameters.description: Destination tensor.

parameters.name: indices
parameters.description:
One integer index tensor per dimension of `input`.  All
broadcast to a common shape.

parameters.name: values
parameters.description: Values to scatter, broadcastable to the common index shape.

parameters.name: accumulate
parameters.description: If True, add at each position; otherwise overwrite.

members.name: argwhere
members.summary: Return the coordinates of every non-zero element in `x`.
members.extended:
Thin alias for `lucid.nonzero` named to match the NumPy /
reference-framework convention.  The output is a synchronisation
point on GPU streams — the kernel can't know how many non-zeros
there are without a device→host count.

parameters.name: x
parameters.description:
Input tensor of any shape and dtype; zero is determined by the
usual truthiness rules (`0` for numeric dtypes, `False` for
bool).

returns.description:
Shape `(N, x.ndim)` `int64` tensor; row `i` lists the
multi-dimensional index of the `i`-th non-zero element in
row-major order.

members.name: scatter_reduce
members.signature: scatter_reduce(input: Tensor, dim: int, index: Tensor, src: Tensor, reduce: str = 'sum', include_self: bool = True)
members.summary: Reduce `src` into `input` along `dim` at positions given by `index`.
members.extended:
Multi-reduction sibling of `scatter_add`.  When several entries
of `src` target the same position the chosen `reduce` op decides
how they combine.  `include_self` controls whether the existing
value in `input` participates in the reduction or is replaced.

parameters.name: input
parameters.description: Destination tensor; not mutated (a fresh copy is returned).

parameters.name: dim
parameters.description: Axis along which `index` / `src` are scattered.

parameters.name: index
parameters.description:
Integer tensor broadcasting against `src`; each entry names
the position along `dim` of `input` to update.

parameters.name: src
parameters.description:
Values to scatter into `input` at the positions named by
`index`.

parameters.name: reduce
parameters.description:
Reduction op applied when multiple `src` values collide on
the same target.  One of `'sum'` (default), `'mean'`,
`'prod'`, `'amax'`, `'amin'`.

parameters.name: include_self
parameters.description:
When `True` (default) the existing value in `input` is part
of the reduction set; when `False` it is overwritten and only
the scattered values count.

returns.description: Same shape and dtype as `input`.
